messages.content: Welcome to your reentry intake assessment. I'll be asking you questions about different aspects of your reentry planning.
Let's start with some basic information. Do you have a birth certificate?
Yes, I have a birth certificate.
Great! Do you have a social security card?
Yes, I have that too.
Do you have a government-issued ID or driver's license issued within the past 10 years?
I have a driver's license but it was suspended.
Are you a veteran?
No, I'm not a veteran.
What transportation do you expect to have after release?
I'll need to use public transportation since my license is suspended. My sister said she can give me rides sometimes.
Do you have any outstanding warrants or detainers?
No warrants that I know of.
Is this your first time being released from prison?
No, this is my second time. Last time I struggled with finding stable housing and that led to other problems.
Now let's talk about housing. Do you have a confirmed place to go immediately upon release?
Yes, my sister said I can stay with her for a few weeks while I get back on my feet.
Who will be living there with you?
My sister, her husband, and their two kids.
Have you discussed this arrangement with them and what the expectations are?
Yes, we talked about it. I need to help with groceries and keep the place clean. No drinking or drugs in the house.
How long can you stay there?
She said a month, maybe two if things go well and I'm looking for my own place.
What's your backup plan if this housing falls through?
I looked into the halfway house downtown. I could apply there if needed.
Do you plan to work after release?
YES, definitely. I need to work to support myself.
Do you already have a job lined up?
Not yet, but I have some leads. I worked construction before and there's always work if you show up on time.
How many hours per week are you looking to work?
Full time, 40 hours or more if possible. I need the money.
Do you have a resume prepared?
No, I need help with that. I never really had a formal one before.
Let's talk about education. What's the highest level of education you've completed?
I finished high school, got my diploma before I came in here.
Have you completed any educational or vocational programs while incarcerated?
Yeah, I took some GED prep classes to help other guys, and I did a welding program last year.
Are you interested in continuing education after release?
Maybe community college eventually. I'd like to learn more about electrical work.
Now let's discuss finances. Do you have any court-ordered financial obligations like restitution or fines?
Yes, I owe about $2,000 in restitution and some court fees.
Do you have child support obligations?
Yes, for my daughter. It's been suspended while I was in here but it'll start back up.
Have you applied for Medicaid or other health insurance?
Not yet, but I know I need to do that right away.
Let's talk about family. Who is part of your primary family?
My sister Sarah that I mentioned, my daughter Emma who's 8, and my mom, though we don't talk much.
Do you feel your sister is supportive?
Yes, very much so. She's been writing me letters and visiting when she can.
What are your goals regarding your relationship with your daughter?
I want to be a better father. I've missed two years of her life. I want to see her regularly and maybe eventually have more custody.
Are there any court orders affecting your contact with family members?
I have supervised visitation with my daughter for now. Her mom and I have to go through the court.
Who are the most supportive people in your life right now?
My sister for sure, and there's a guy I met in here, Marcus, who gets out next month. We've been talking about staying in touch and helping each other stay on track.
Are you interested in developing new positive social connections?
Yes, I think I need to. Most of my old friends aren't good influences. I'd like to find people who are focused on positive things.
What activities do you enjoy in your free time?
I like working out, playing basketball, and I got into reading while I was in here. I also like fixing things with my hands.
How do you plan to participate in these activities after release?
There's a community center near my sister's place that has a gym and basketball court. I want to get a library card too.
Are there any former activities you plan to avoid?
Yeah, hanging out at bars and parties. That's where I got into trouble before.
Finally, let's discuss your relationship with alcohol and drugs. Do you have a history with substance abuse?
Yes, I had a drinking problem. That's what led to my charges - I made bad decisions when I was drinking.
Have you participated in any substance abuse treatment programs?
I did AA meetings in here for about a year. It helped me understand my problem better.
Are you interested in continuing treatment support after release?
Yes, I want to keep going to AA meetings. I know I can't do this alone.
Thank you for completing your intake assessment. This information will help us better support your reentry planning.
